items.claim: Alessandro Costa's low stance and short stature make him difficult to take down.
items.quote: He also fights out of kind of a low stance, and being 5'4 makes him hard to shoot takedowns on with ease and drag to the ground.

items.claim: Cody Durden will struggle to chain takedowns against Alessandro Costa.
items.quote: I think Cody Durden is going to have a hard time just putting the takedowns together and threatening in that area substantially with Costa.

items.claim: Alessandro Costa possesses superior boxing and kickboxing compared to Cody Durden.
items.quote: I favor Costa's, you know, boxing kickboxing style, the heavy low kicks, the powerful overhand shots, make Costa a bit of a nightmare match-up for Cody Durden.

items.claim: Ryan Gandara's punching power is significantly greater than Zac Reese's.
items.quote: I really think the punching power is a massive difference maker between the two of the fighters here.

items.claim: Zac Reese is susceptible to being pressured backwards and countered by power strikers.
items.quote: I think he's going to be getting pressured backwards and, you know, for a kicker it's hard to just be chopping those power shots to the body and head, etc., or even the legs as you're getting walked down and countered with big punches.

items.claim: Zac Reese lacks exceptional wrestling and physical strength.
items.quote: not exceptional with his wrestling, not crazy physical.

items.claim: Ryan Gandara is a more efficient finisher than Zac Reese based on their common opponent, Jose Medina.
items.quote: Let's not forget though, Zac Reese went three hard rounds against Jose Medina. Gandulla got him in, you know, what? 30 or 40 seconds?
